name: tc_kep_f1_circular_leo_full_period
description: Circular LEO at r = 6778000 m (400 km altitude), propagate one full orbital period. Kepler's third law: T = 2*pi*sqrt(r^3/mu). r^3 = 6778000^3 = 3.11297e20 m^3. r^3/mu = 3.11297e20 / 3.986004418e14 = 781022 s^2. sqrt(781022) = 883.75 s. T = 2*pi*883.75 = 5552.4 s. Initial state: r0 = [6778000, 0, 0] m. Circular speed: v_circ = sqrt(mu/r) = sqrt(3.986004418e14/6778000) = sqrt(58811000) = 7668.8 m/s. v0 = [0, 7668.8, 0] m/s. After exactly one orbital period, the two-body Keplerian orbit returns to the identical state: r1 = r0, v1 = v0. The universal-variable solver preserves this exactly modulo floating-point round-trip error. Battin §4.3: the Lagrange coefficients satisfy f*g_dot - f_dot*g = 1 (identity), so after period T, chi = 2*pi*sqrt(a^3/mu)*sqrt(mu) = 2*pi*sqrt(a) (universal anomaly completes one revolution). For a circular orbit a = r, so chi_1period = 2*pi*sqrt(r) = 2*pi*sqrt(6778000) = 2*pi*2603.5 = 16360 m^0.5 (non-standard unit). The round-trip invariant holds to machine precision. Position tolerance 10 m, velocity tolerance 0.01 m/s to allow for floating-point accumulation in the Newton-Raphson loop.
note: Full-period round-trip invariance. Expected state = initial state. 10 m / 0.01 m/s tolerance to allow floating-point Newton-Raphson accumulation. Kepler third law T = 2*pi*sqrt(r^3/mu) = 5552.4 s.

name: tc_kep_f2_circular_leo_half_period
description: Circular LEO at r = 6778000 m, propagate half an orbital period. Half-period: T/2 = 5552.4/2 = 2776.2 s. Initial state: r0 = [6778000, 0, 0], v0 = [0, 7668.8, 0]. After half an orbit the spacecraft has moved 180 deg around the orbit. For a circular orbit starting at [r,0,0] with prograde velocity [0,v_circ,0], after half a period: position = [-r, 0, 0] = [-6778000, 0, 0]. velocity = [0, -v_circ, 0] = [0, -7668.8, 0]. This follows directly from uniform circular motion: position rotates by pi radians, velocity rotates by pi radians. The prograde velocity [0, v_circ, 0] at angle 0 becomes [0, -v_circ, 0] at angle pi. The Lagrange f-coefficient: f = 1 - chi^2/r0 * C(z). At t=T/2, the universal variable satisfies chi_half = sqrt(mu) * T/2 / r (first-order) = 7668.8 * 2776.2 / 6778000 (non-dimensional). Full calculation is implicit in the Newton-Raphson loop; the result must be [-r, 0, 0] and [0, -v_circ, 0] as established by the symmetry of the circular orbit.
note: Half-period symmetry. After T/2 the position is anti-podal (-r,0,0) and velocity is reversed (0,-v_circ,0). Exact from circular orbit symmetry. 10 m / 0.01 m/s tolerance.

name: tc_kep_f3_circular_leo_quarter_period
description: Circular LEO at r = 6778000 m, propagate one quarter orbital period. Quarter period: T/4 = 5552.4/4 = 1388.1 s. Initial state: r0 = [6778000, 0, 0], v0 = [0, 7668.8, 0]. After a quarter orbit (90 deg of true anomaly) on a circular orbit starting at [r,0,0] moving prograde, the position is [0, r, 0] = [0, 6778000, 0]. Velocity has rotated 90 deg from [0, v_circ, 0] to [-v_circ, 0, 0] = [-7668.8, 0, 0]. Derivation: circular orbit parametric equations x(t) = r*cos(n*t), y(t) = r*sin(n*t) where n = v_circ/r = 7668.8/6778000 = 1.13136e-3 rad/s. At t = T/4: n*t = (1.13136e-3)*(1388.1) = 1.57080 rad = pi/2. x = r*cos(pi/2) = 0. y = r*sin(pi/2) = 6778000. vx = -v_circ*sin(pi/2) = -7668.8. vy = v_circ*cos(pi/2) = 0. Result: position = [0, 6778000, 0], velocity = [-7668.8, 0, 0].
note: Quarter-period 90-deg rotation. Position rotates to +y axis; velocity rotates to -x. Pure circular-orbit parametric equations, Battin §2.1.

name: tc_kep_f4_elliptic_periapsis_to_apoapsis
description: Elliptic orbit with e = 0.1, propagate from periapsis to apoapsis (half period). Semi-major axis a = 8000000 m (arbitrary choice). Periapsis radius: r_peri = a*(1-e) = 8000000*(1-0.1) = 7200000 m. Apoapsis radius: r_apo = a*(1+e) = 8000000*(1+0.1) = 8800000 m. Periapsis speed (vis-viva): v_peri = sqrt(mu*(2/r_peri - 1/a)) = sqrt(3.986004418e14*(2/7200000 - 1/8000000)). 2/7200000 = 2.77778e-7; 1/8000000 = 1.25e-7; diff = 1.52778e-7. v_peri = sqrt(3.986004418e14 * 1.52778e-7) = sqrt(60914000) = 7805.4 m/s. Apoapsis speed (vis-viva): v_apo = sqrt(mu*(2/r_apo - 1/a)) = sqrt(3.986004418e14*(2/8800000 - 1/8000000)). 2/8800000 = 2.27273e-7; 1/8000000 = 1.25e-7; diff = 1.02273e-7. v_apo = sqrt(3.986004418e14 * 1.02273e-7) = sqrt(40756000) = 6384.1 m/s. Half period: T/2 = pi*sqrt(a^3/mu) = pi*sqrt(8000000^3/3.986004418e14). a^3 = 5.12e20. a^3/mu = 5.12e20/3.986004418e14 = 1284500. sqrt(1284500) = 1133.4. T/2 = pi*1133.4 = 3560.7 s. Initial state: r0 = [7200000, 0, 0] (periapsis on x-axis), v0 = [0, 7805.4, 0] (prograde tangential). After T/2, the spacecraft reaches apoapsis. For a prograde orbit starting at periapsis [r_peri, 0, 0], the apoapsis is at [-r_apo, 0, 0] = [-8800000, 0, 0]. Velocity at apoapsis is [0, -v_apo, 0] = [0, -6384.1, 0] (prograde tangential at apoapsis, which points in the -y direction when the orbit has rotated pi radians from the x-axis). Energy check: E = 0.5*v_peri^2 - mu/r_peri = 0.5*60914000 - 3.986004418e14/7200000 = 30457000 - 55361200 = -24904200 J/kg. E = -mu/(2a) = -3.986004418e14/(2*8000000) = -24912500 J/kg. Agreement within rounding.
note: Elliptic orbit e=0.1, periapsis to apoapsis. v_peri = 7805.4 m/s, v_apo = 6384.1 m/s from vis-viva. Half-period T/2 = pi*sqrt(a^3/mu) = 3560.7 s. Position at apoapsis is [-r_apo, 0, 0].

name: tc_kep_f5_elliptic_quarter_period_from_periapsis
note: Elliptic e=0.1 orbit, T/4 from periapsis. True anomaly at T/4 derived by solving M=pi/2 via Kepler's equation (Newton iteration converging to E=1.6705 rad, nu=101.40 deg). Position r=8077800 m. Speed 6991.2 m/s from vis-viva. Wider tolerance (100 m, 0.1 m/s) because the Kepler equation numerical inversion accumulates intermediate rounding.

name: tc_kep_f6_llo_full_period
note: LLO full-period round-trip. Uses MU_MOON = 4.902800118e12 m^3/s^2. T = 7068.6 s. Same round-trip invariant as TC-KEP-F1.

name: tc_kep_f7_leo_300km_60s_short_step
note: Short 60 s step on 300 km circular orbit. Expected state from exact circular orbit parametric equations (angle theta = v_circ*dt/r). 1 m / 0.01 m/s tight tolerance appropriate for the short step.

name: tc_kep_f8_inclined_iss_like_half_period
description: ISS-like 400 km circular orbit, inclination 51.6 deg, half period. r = 6778000 m. v_circ = 7668.8 m/s. T/2 = 2776.2 s. For an inclined orbit the initial position is on the equatorial x-axis and the velocity has both y and z components. Inclination i = 51.6 deg = 0.90058 rad. The orbit plane has angular momentum h perpendicular to the orbit plane. For inclination i and ascending node at the x-axis (RAAN = 0), the initial state at the ascending node is: r0 = [6778000, 0, 0]. v0 = v_circ * [0, cos(i), sin(i)] = 7668.8 * [0, cos(51.6), sin(51.6)]. cos(51.6 deg) = cos(0.90058 rad). 51.6 deg = 51 + 0.6 deg. cos(51 deg) = 0.62932; correction for 0.6 deg: cos(51.6 deg) = cos(51 deg)*cos(0.6 deg) - sin(51 deg)*sin(0.6 deg) = 0.62932*0.99995 - 0.77714*0.010472 = 0.62929 - 0.00814 = 0.62115. sin(51.6 deg): sin(51 deg) = 0.77714; sin(51.6 deg) = sin(51)*cos(0.6) + cos(51)*sin(0.6) = 0.77714*0.99995 + 0.62932*0.010472 = 0.77710 + 0.00659 = 0.78369. v0 = 7668.8 * [0, 0.62115, 0.78369] = [0, 4763.0, 6008.2] m/s. After half a period on this inclined circular orbit, the spacecraft is diametrically opposite. The angular momentum vector h = r0 x v0: h = [6778000, 0, 0] x [0, 4763.0, 6008.2] = [0*6008.2 - 0*4763.0, 0*0 - 6778000*6008.2, 6778000*4763.0 - 0*0] = [0, -40720600, 32281000] m^2/s. After half a period, the position rotates by pi around the orbit normal. For the initial position r0 = [r, 0, 0] and the orbit normal h_hat = [0, -sin(i), cos(i)] = [0, -0.78369, 0.62115], the rotation by pi gives r1 = -r0 + 2*(r0 . h_hat)*h_hat. r0 . h_hat = 6778000*(0) + 0*(-0.78369) + 0*(0.62115) = 0. Therefore r1 = -r0 = [-6778000, 0, 0]. Velocity at apoapsis (diametrically opposite for circular orbit): v1 = -v0 rotated by pi around h_hat = [0, -v_circ*cos(i), -v_circ*sin(i)] = [0, -4763.0, -6008.2]. The rotation formula for a vector rotated 180 deg around axis h_hat: v_rotated = 2*(v . h_hat)*h_hat - v. v0 . h_hat = 0*0 + 4763.0*(-0.78369) + 6008.2*(0.62115) = -3734.0 + 3732.5 = -1.5 ~ 0 (numerical noise; this is zero by construction since v0 is in the orbit plane and perpendicular to h_hat). v1 = 2*(-1.5)*[0, -0.78369, 0.62115] - [0, 4763.0, 6008.2] = ~ [0, -4763.0, -6008.2] m/s.
note: ISS-like 51.6-deg inclination, half-period. The half-period symmetry holds regardless of inclination: position negates, velocity negates. Inclination preserved in the z-component of both position (zero at equatorial crossing) and velocity. Tests 3D orbit propagation.

name: tc_kep_f9_elliptic_e02_full_period
note: Elliptic e=0.2 full-period round-trip. r_peri=8000000 m, r_apo=12000000 m. v_peri=7732.4 m/s from vis-viva. T=9953.5 s. Tests round-trip invariance for non-circular elliptic orbit.
